title: Ancient Andes
description: Test your knowledge of the ancient Andean civilizations!

assessment_items.question_content:
Which of  the following best explains why some people would not consider the Caral a civilization?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The Caral pyramids were monumental structures.

choices.content: They probably obtained most of their food from the sea, rather than by agriculture
They did not build monumental structures

assessment_items.question_content:
The temple at Chavín de Huantar 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The temple at Chavín de Huantar had a religious, not political, purpose.

choices.content: was used primarily for political events
used advanced construction and drainage techniques
was reserved only for the use of elites

assessment_items.question_content:
The reason that the Chavín were able to grow both potatoes and maize was that

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Potatoes and maize do not grow well in the same climate.

choices.content: both crops grow well in the same climate
they moved seasonally to grow each crop
they had easy access to both low and highland fields

assessment_items.question_content:
Although there is not a clear record of what Chavín government looked like, many scholars believe there was some form of religious hierarchy because 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Not all societies have religious hierarchies.

choices.content: the temple is the only monumental architecture, and only shamans could interpret symbols
there are written records explaining the prominent role of religious leaders in Chavín society

assessment_items.question_content:
The positioning of the Lanzón and the Tello Obelisk seem to indicate the Chavín had advanced knowledge of  

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The Lanzón is a sculpture, not a building that would require architectural knowledge.
